meta.notes: Values in Mt CO2e (million tonnes CO2 equivalent) unless noted. Sector figures are annual. Per-activity figures use kg CO2e. Sources keyed to sources.md.

sectors.label: AI Inference (all gen-AI queries)
sectors.notes: IEA estimate for all generative AI queries globally. Projected 347 TWh by 2030.

sectors.label: AI Model Training
sectors.notes: Training is a one-off cost per model, not annual. GPT-4 estimated ~50 GWh alone. Included here for completeness but not directly comparable to operational figures.

sectors.label: All Data Centres (global)
sectors.notes: Includes AI and non-AI. ~1.5% of global electricity. US (180 TWh) + China (102 TWh) = 69% of total. Projected to reach 945 TWh by 2030.

sectors.label: Bitcoin Mining
sectors.notes: Cambridge CBECI 2025 Digital Mining Industry Report. ~0.5% of global electricity. Some estimates range to 175 TWh.

sectors.label: Video Streaming (Netflix + YouTube)
sectors.notes: Highly disputed figures. Netflix external estimate ~94 TWh/yr (not Netflix's own 0.45 TWh which excludes CDN/ISP/device). YouTube theoretical ~600 TWh/yr at 1B hrs/day. 300 TWh used as conservative combined estimate. Video = 60-70% of global internet traffic.

sectors.label: Cloud & Personal Storage (iCloud, Google Photos etc.)
sectors.notes: No reliable global TWh figure. Per-unit: 3-7 kWh per GB transferred+stored (Carnegie Mellon). ~40-70 kWh per TB per year ongoing. Billions of users storing thousands of photos indefinitely.

sectors.label: Mobile / Cellular Networks
sectors.notes: 2015 baseline (Andrae). Substantially higher now with 5G rollout — 5G uses more energy per tower than 4G. No reliable 2024 global figure found.

sectors.label: Standby / Vampire Power
sectors.notes: IEA: ~5% of global electricity, ~1% of global CO2. Equivalent to ~15 million petrol cars. In developed countries, 5-25% of residential electricity.

sectors.label: Global Aviation
sectors.notes: IATA 2024 data. International flights = ~60% (~550 Mt CO2). Grew ~8% YoY. Does not include non-CO2 warming effects (contrails etc.) which may double effective impact.

sectors.label: Beef & Livestock Farming
sectors.notes: FAO: all livestock supply chains = 7.1 Gt CO2e = 14.5% of global emissions. Cattle (beef+dairy) = ~2/3 of that (~4.7 Gt). Beef alone ~3 Gt CO2e. Emissions from methane (enteric fermentation), feed production, land-use change.

sectors.label: Food Waste
sectors.notes: UNFCCC: 8-10% of global GHG. ~3.3 Gt CO2e (conservative). Nearly 5x aviation. 1/3 of all food produced is lost or wasted. If food waste were a country, it would be 3rd largest emitter.

sectors.label: Mining & Metals Sector
sectors.notes: 11% of global GHG. Steel/aluminium/coal mining = 93% of sector. Asia = 80% of sector emissions. Includes coal, iron ore, bauxite, lithium (EV batteries). 93% classified as Scope 1.

sectors.label: Cement & Concrete Production
sectors.notes: ~7-8% of global CO2. 1.47 Gt CO2 in 2024. 50% from chemical process (calcination of limestone), 40% from fuel burning. Emissions tripled 1990-2020. Most-used material on earth after water.

sectors.label: Fast Fashion & Apparel
sectors.notes: 8-10% of global CO2 emissions. More than aviation + shipping combined. 110 million tonnes of oil equivalent in synthetic fibre production alone. Projected 26% of global emissions by 2050 if unchanged.

sectors.label: Australian Black Summer Bushfires (2019-20)
sectors.notes: Nature study: 715 Mt CO2. Climate Council: ~900 Mt CO2. Australia's normal annual emissions: ~537 Mt CO2e. Black Summer released more CO2 in one season than Australia normally emits in a full year. Partly carbon-cycle neutral long-term (regrowth reabsorbs) but increasing frequency/intensity disrupts this balance.

per_activity.label: One ChatGPT text query
per_activity.notes: ~0.3 Wh per query (IEA / Sam Altman). ~10x a Google search. Image generation is 10-50x higher.

per_activity.label: One Google search
per_activity.notes: ~0.03 Wh per search. Widely cited estimate.

per_activity.label: One hour Netflix streaming
per_activity.notes: IEA/Carbon Brief corrected figure. Netflix's own figure (100g CO2/hr) excludes network and device energy.

per_activity.label: 1 kg beef (production)
per_activity.notes: Average across beef herd. Equivalent to ~333 ChatGPT queries.

per_activity.label: One transatlantic flight (economy, per passenger)
per_activity.notes: London–New York return, economy class. Equivalent to ~5 million ChatGPT queries.

per_activity.label: 1 TB cloud storage (per year)
per_activity.notes: Mid-range estimate: 40-70 kWh/TB/year. Does not include upload/download energy.

per_activity.label: 10-minute hot shower
per_activity.notes: Electric water heater. Gas heater roughly half. One of the largest single household energy uses.
